query: Argentina 2026 budget labor tax penal code reform bill Congress vote status latest
a_issues: Result set A is incomplete and mixes statuses from different times. It does not directly include the key official Senate approval result for the 2026 budget or the latest final status. One labor-law result is internally inconsistent: the URL says 2026/03/23 but the snippet says Congress approved the law on 27 February 2027, which is likely erroneous. Reuters and Buenos Aires Times discuss the agenda for special sessions rather than final vote outcomes. The tax-law snippet refers to enactment under a labor modernization law but does not clearly state the broader congressional vote status of the queried tax bill. Penal-code reform is described only as planned/tougher penalties, not final status.
b_issues: Result set B is largely irrelevant to the query. It contains no usable Argentina-specific congressional vote-status reporting on the 2026 budget, labor reform, tax reform, or penal-code reform. Several results are about unrelated U.S. congressional materials or generic country background, so an LLM would be unable to answer the question from B alone.
reasoning: A is clearly better because it at least contains relevant Argentina reform items tied to Congress: Reuters says special sessions were called to push the 2026 budget, labor changes, tax 'presumption of innocence' reform, and penal-code reform; Buenos Aires Times gives the same agenda; EY says tax amendments were enacted under a labor modernization law; and one labor-law source indicates a labor reform was approved. Although A is imperfect and misses the strongest/latest official budget vote facts from the anchor, it provides some answerable status signals. B provides essentially no relevant facts about the queried Argentine bills or their vote status, so it would not enable a correct answer.
